revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二所神社 (姫路市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二所神社 (姫路市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二所神社 (姫路市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二所神社 (姫路市)]]
{{Unreferenced|date=May 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Jūnisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Jyunisyo Shrine Himeji.JPG |location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}} Jūnisho-mae-chō 120 |coordinates = {{Coord|34.830028|N|134.68625|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} |Rank = |Established = {{ill|928|en|928|qid=Q24297|lt=928|1=928}} ({{ill|Engi era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379316}} 6th year) |MainHall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |AnnualFestival = October 9 |Rituals = Okiku Festival (May 8) |location = {{ill|Himeji|qid=Q209801|1=Himeji}} }}
'''Jūnisho Shrine''' (十二所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The shrine is dedicated to the deity of medicine, and its blessings include recovery from illness. Its auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Okiku Shrine|ja|お菊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okiku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十二所神社 (姫路市)|1=Juuni-sho Shrine (Himeji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17214620}}, is dedicated to Okiku, the protagonist of the {{ill|Banshū Sarayashiki|ja|播州皿屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banshū Sarayashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皿屋敷|1=Banchō Sarayashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1369219}} legend.
== deity ==
The main deity of the shrine is {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|スクナヒコナ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}.
== Overview ==
According to the shrine's legend, during an outbreak of {{ill|epidemic|ja|疫病|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/epidemic|1=ekibyou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17239211}} in {{ill|928|en|928|qid=Q24297|lt=928|1=928}} ({{ill|Engi era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379316}} 6th year), twelve stalks of {{ill|Artemisia princeps|en|Artemisia princeps|qid=Q1076280|lt=mugwort|1=Artemisia princeps}} miraculously sprouted overnight. {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} appeared and declared that the mugwort should be used to cure the epidemic. Following this divine guidance, the villagers' illnesses were healed, and they established a shrine to enshrine Sukunahikona in Nanpe-machi, Daishōgun.
In {{ill|1175|en|1175|qid=Q19692|lt=1175|1=1175}} ({{ill|Angen|en|Angen|qid=Q1193287|lt=Angen|1=Angen}} 1st year), the shrine was relocated to its current site. Another tradition states that the shrine was moved to its current location during the {{ill|Himeji Domain|en|Himeji Domain|qid=Q1062190|lt=Himeji Domain|1=Himeji Domain}} period to serve as a protective shrine for the {{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}} (southwest) of {{ill|Himeji Castle|en|Himeji Castle|qid=Q188754|lt=Himeji Castle|1=Himeji Castle}}.
In {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), the shrine suffered damage during the {{ill|Himeji Air Raid|ja|姫路空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Himeji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11447565}}, resulting in a reduced precinct size. Reflecting the twelve mugwort stalks, the shrine is named Jūnisho Shrine, and its crest features a mugwort leaf. The shrine's hall faces east, which is a unique characteristic. The surrounding area has been named Jūnisho-mae-chō, and the nearby municipal road, part of {{ill|Japan National Route 2|en|Japan National Route 2|qid=Q1131340|lt=Route 2 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 2}}, is called the {{ill|Himeji Municipal Jūnisho-mae Line|ja|姫路市道十二所前線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji Municipal Jūnisho-mae Line|1=Himeji Municipal Jūnisho-mae Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11447550}}.
== Okiku Shrine ==
[[File:Okiku-shrine.jpg|thumb|250px|Okiku Shrine]] The enshrined deity is "Kikuhime-no-Mikoto" (Okiku). According to local traditions, Okiku worshipped at Jūnisho Shrine, leading to her veneration. The overlap of {{ill|Artemisia princeps|en|Artemisia princeps|qid=Q1076280|lt=mugwort|1=Artemisia princeps}} and {{ill|Chrysanthemum × morifolium|en|Chrysanthemum × morifolium|qid=Q15530181|lt=chrysanthemum|1=Chrysanthemum × morifolium}} as medicinal plants, combined with Okiku's association with medicine, contributed to the creation of the auxiliary shrine, {{ill|Okiku Shrine|ja|お菊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okiku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十二所神社 (姫路市)|1=Juuni-sho Shrine (Himeji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17214620}}.
== location ==
Jūnisho-mae-chō 120, Himeji, Hyōgo Prefecture.
== Access ==
From {{ill|Himeji Station|en|Himeji Station|qid=Q119301|lt=Himeji Station|1=Himeji Station}} on the {{ill|San'yō Main Line|en|San'yō Main Line|qid=Q1193436|lt=San'yō Main Line|1=San'yō Main Line}} or {{ill|Sanyo-Himeji Station|en|Sanyo-Himeji Station|qid=Q7420806|lt=San'yō Himeji Station|1=Sanyo-Himeji Station}} of the {{ill|San'yō Electric Railway|ja|山陽電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'yō Electric Railway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陽電気鉄道|1=Sanyo Electric Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2245513}}, walk approximately 400 meters west from the Shiragin Intersection on {{ill|Ōtemae-dōri|ja|大手前通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtemae-dōri|1=Ōtemae-dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11435552}}.
== Related Articles ==
